elements.title: Charlotte Brontë
elements.contents: ... that the first novel by Charlotte Brontë (pictured) was published posthumously?

elements.title: Amor vien dal destino
elements.contents: ... that Agostino Steffani missed the 1709 premiere of his opera Amor vien dal destino because he was in Rome mediating between the pope and the emperor?

elements.title: John H. Beyer
elements.contents: ... that John H. Beyer co-founded an architectural firm in 1968 that opposed large-scale urban redevelopment, inspired partly by the book The Death and Life of Great American Cities?

elements.title: Bird of Pray
elements.contents: ... that the Eurovision performance of "Bird of Pray" featured a backing vocalist whose song had been defeated during selection?

elements.title: 2022 LastPass data breach
elements.contents: ... that a key moment in the 2022 LastPass data breach saw an attacker compromise an engineer's Plex account?

elements.title: Bob Broughton
elements.contents: ... that cameraman Bob Broughton, who later became a Disney Legend, was first hired there by mistake?

elements.title: Running Up That Hill
elements.contents: ... that more than 2,000 children's artworks were used in the music video for Meg Myers's cover of "Running Up That Hill"?

elements.title: the mayor of El Paso, Texas
elements.contents: ... that the mayor of El Paso, Texas, founded a tequila company that sells its product in dog-shaped bottles?

elements.title: her favourite rock
elements.contents: ... that a five-year-old girl donated her favourite rock to Poole Museum, after which it was put on display and became the museum's most famous object?
